────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  sevro   Helm chart cost & security analysis
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  source: /home/shivam/Desktop/Merge/sevro/testdata/fixtures/basic-chart/values.yaml
  10 workloads · 31 findings

   HIGH   admin-tool
    allowPrivilegeEscalation explicitly enabled
    securityContext.allowPrivilegeEscalation=true lets a process gain
    capabilities its parent didn't have (setuid, file capabilities). Set
    allowPrivilegeEscalation=false unless you're running an init system or
    known-good legacy binary that requires it.
    confidence: ●●● high

   HIGH   admin-tool
    Container declared privileged
    securityContext.privileged=true bypasses every Linux capability check.
    Privileged is correct only for node-level agents (CSI drivers, node
    exporters); application workloads should set privileged=false and request
    the specific capabilities they actually need.
    confidence: ●●● high

   HIGH   admin-tool
    Container runs as root
    securityContext.runAsNonRoot is set to false. Running as UID 0 expands the
    blast radius of any container escape. Set runAsNonRoot=true and runAsUser
    to a non-zero UID.
    confidence: ●●● high

   MED    api
    allowPrivilegeEscalation not declared
    securityContext.allowPrivilegeEscalation is undeclared; Kubernetes
    defaults it to true. Set allowPrivilegeEscalation=false to make the
    no_new_privs bit explicit.
    confidence: ●●○ medium

   MED    api   save ~$29.20 /mo
    CPU request appears overprovisioned
    Request 2 vs limit 2.5 — typical utilization rarely justifies this
    ratio. Consider halving the request.
    confidence: ●●○ medium

   MED    api   save ~$2.79 /mo
    Memory request appears overprovisioned
    Request 1.6Gi vs limit 2Gi — typical utilization rarely justifies this
    ratio. Consider halving the request.
    confidence: ●●○ medium

   MED    api
    runAsNonRoot not declared
    securityContext.runAsNonRoot is not declared. Without it, Kubernetes
    defers to the image's USER directive — which is UID 0 for most public
    images. Set runAsNonRoot=true to make the intent explicit.
    confidence: ●●○ medium

   LOW    api
    readOnlyRootFilesystem not declared
    securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem is not declared. Declare
    readOnlyRootFilesystem=true to make hardening explicit; mount tmpfs
    volumes for legitimate write paths.
    confidence: ●●○ medium

   MED    bursty
    CPU limit is many multiples of the request
    Request 200m vs limit 2 — a 10.0x burst window. The scheduler reserves
    only the request, so the pod can suddenly consume far more than its
    node-allocation suggests. Tighten the limit or raise the request to match
    observed P95.
    confidence: ●●○ medium

   MED    bursty
    Memory limit is many multiples of the request
    Request 256Mi vs limit 4Gi — a 16.0x burst window. Memory limits aren't
    elastic the way CPU is: a pod that climbs to its limit will OOM, taking
    neighbours down via node memory pressure. Tighten the limit toward
    observed P95.
    confidence: ●●○ medium

   MED    cache
    allowPrivilegeEscalation not declared
    securityContext.allowPrivilegeEscalation is undeclared; Kubernetes
    defaults it to true. Set allowPrivilegeEscalation=false to make the
    no_new_privs bit explicit.
    confidence: ●●○ medium

   MED    cache
    CPU limit is many multiples of the request
    Request 100m vs limit 500m — a 5.0x burst window. The scheduler reserves
    only the request, so the pod can suddenly consume far more than its
    node-allocation suggests. Tighten the limit or raise the request to match
    observed P95.
    confidence: ●●○ medium

   MED    cache
    Image not pinned to a stable tag
    Image redis:latest uses :latest. The same tag can resolve to different
    bytes across deploys; rollback becomes ambiguous. Pin to an immutable
    digest or version tag.
    confidence: ●●● high

   LOW    cache
    readOnlyRootFilesystem not declared
    securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem is not declared. Declare
    readOnlyRootFilesystem=true to make hardening explicit; mount tmpfs
    volumes for legitimate write paths.
    confidence: ●●○ medium

   MED    clean
    CPU limit is many multiples of the request
    Request 100m vs limit 500m — a 5.0x burst window. The scheduler reserves
    only the request, so the pod can suddenly consume far more than its
    node-allocation suggests. Tighten the limit or raise the request to match
    observed P95.
    confidence: ●●○ medium

   MED    dev-build
    Floating image tag prevents reproducible deploys
    Image tag 'main' is a floating reference: upstream re-points it whenever
    they publish. The same Helm release can therefore land different bytes on
    consecutive deploys, and rollbacks become ambiguous. Pin to an immutable
    digest or version tag.
    confidence: ●●● high

   LOW    dev-build
    readOnlyRootFilesystem not declared
    securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem is not declared. Declare
    readOnlyRootFilesystem=true to make hardening explicit; mount tmpfs
    volumes for legitimate write paths.
    confidence: ●●○ medium

   MED    logger
    allowPrivilegeEscalation not declared
    securityContext.allowPrivilegeEscalation is undeclared; Kubernetes
    defaults it to true. Set allowPrivilegeEscalation=false to make the
    no_new_privs bit explicit.
    confidence: ●●○ medium

   MED    logger
    CPU limit is many multiples of the request
    Request 100m vs limit 500m — a 5.0x burst window. The scheduler reserves
    only the request, so the pod can suddenly consume far more than its
    node-allocation suggests. Tighten the limit or raise the request to match
    observed P95.
    confidence: ●●○ medium

   MED    logger
    Image not pinned to a stable tag
    Image fluentd uses (no tag). The same tag can resolve to different bytes
    across deploys; rollback becomes ambiguous. Pin to an immutable digest or
    version tag.
    confidence: ●●● high

   LOW    logger
    readOnlyRootFilesystem not declared
    securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem is not declared. Declare
    readOnlyRootFilesystem=true to make hardening explicit; mount tmpfs
    volumes for legitimate write paths.
    confidence: ●●○ medium

   MED    shipper
    allowPrivilegeEscalation not declared
    securityContext.allowPrivilegeEscalation is undeclared; Kubernetes
    defaults it to true. Set allowPrivilegeEscalation=false to make the
    no_new_privs bit explicit.
    confidence: ●●○ medium

   MED    shipper
    CPU limit is many multiples of the request
    Request 100m vs limit 500m — a 5.0x burst window. The scheduler reserves
    only the request, so the pod can suddenly consume far more than its
    node-allocation suggests. Tighten the limit or raise the request to match
    observed P95.
    confidence: ●●○ medium

   MED    shipper
    High static replica count without an autoscaler
    Replicas set to 10 without an HPA. Static high replica counts pay for
    unused capacity 24/7. Either enable autoscaling.enabled=true or right-size
    the static count to your observed P95.
    confidence: ●●○ medium

   LOW    shipper
    readOnlyRootFilesystem not declared
    securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem is not declared. Declare
    readOnlyRootFilesystem=true to make hardening explicit; mount tmpfs
    volumes for legitimate write paths.
    confidence: ●●○ medium

   HIGH   sniffer
    Pod uses host network
    hostNetwork=true puts the pod in the node's network namespace, bypassing
    NetworkPolicy and exposing localhost services on the host. Required for
    some CNI plugins and ingress controllers; otherwise leave it unset.
    confidence: ●●● high

   HIGH   sniffer
    Pod mounts a hostPath volume
    A volume of type hostPath exposes the node's filesystem to the pod.
    Required for some node-level agents (log collectors, CNI helpers) but a
    sharp edge for application workloads — a path-traversal bug suddenly
    becomes a node compromise. Replace with PVC, configMap, or downward API
    where possible.
    confidence: ●●● high

   HIGH   worker
    Memory limit not set
    This workload declares a memory request but no limit. Without a limit it
    can consume node memory unbounded and evict neighbours under pressure. Set
    a limit at or above your observed P95.
    confidence: ●●● high

   MED    worker
    allowPrivilegeEscalation not declared
    securityContext.allowPrivilegeEscalation is undeclared; Kubernetes
    defaults it to true. Set allowPrivilegeEscalation=false to make the
    no_new_privs bit explicit.
    confidence: ●●○ medium

   LOW    worker
    CPU limit not set
    This workload declares a CPU request but no limit. Some teams omit CPU
    limits intentionally to avoid throttling; others expect predictable
    scheduling. Confirm this is the intended posture.
    confidence: ●●○ medium

   LOW    worker
    readOnlyRootFilesystem not declared
    securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem is not declared. Declare
    readOnlyRootFilesystem=true to make hardening explicit; mount tmpfs
    volumes for legitimate write paths.
    confidence: ●●○ medium

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  estimated monthly savings: $31.99 /mo   (±40%)
  Sandbox accuracy: ±40%. Install the Sevro agent for exact numbers (sevro.dev/get).
  → install the agent for exact numbers: sevro.dev/get (https://sevro.dev/get)
